step_a.description: Forward verification: PART2 claims checked against SOT

details.claim: PART2 §6.5: 3-Layer Guardrails: L1=NeMo (input), L2=Guardrails AI (output verification), L3=LlamaGuard (safety classification, GPU, V2)
details.note: L2 role is 'processing defense' (처리 방어) in D2.0-07 not 'output verification'. PART2 slightly mischaracterizes L2. Already noted in phase2 F-06.
RBAC roles match exactly across PART2, D2.0-07 §13, and D2.0-08 §8
4 levels (FULL_MANUAL, SUPERVISED, SEMI_AUTO, FULL_AUTO) match with default L1(SUPERVISED)
10min general timeout confirmed in D2.0-07. Note: P2 HITL has 5min timeout (D2.0-07 §2.1 P2 HITL)

details.claim: PART2 §6.5: Cost ceiling V1=40K/mo, V2=93K/mo, V3=266K/mo

details.note: LOCK confirmed: 80% warning(force_mini) / 100% block(deny)

details.claim: PART2 §6.1.6: UI State Machine has 9 states (UI_S0_BOOT through UI_S8_ARCHIVED)
details.note: 9 states match exactly: UI_S0_BOOT, UI_S1_IDLE, UI_S2_EDITING, UI_S3_READY, UI_S4_RUNNING, UI_S5_AWAIT_APPROVAL, UI_S6_PRESENTING, UI_S7_RECOVERY, UI_S8_ARCHIVED
Layout dimensions match: Left 250-300px, Center flex-grow, Right 350-400px

details.claim: PART2 §6.1.2: React components ~44 in V1 across 11 groups
details.note: Component count and groups consistent with D2.0-08 view structures
PART2 explicitly notes SOURCE_CONFLICT (B1 §5.1=47 vs CLAUDE.md=72) and adopts 72 as canonical
13 methods listed explicitly

details.claim: PART2 §6.9: SDAR 7-state machine: IDLE, DETECTING, DIAGNOSING, PRESCRIBING, REPAIRING, VERIFYING, ESCALATED
details.note: SDAR_SPEC uses formal names (SDAR_S0_MONITORING etc.) and PART2 uses simplified names. PART2 XREF note acknowledges this. Phase2 F-09 already flagged as LOW.

details.claim: PART2 §6.9: SDAR 5-Layer pipeline (DETECTION, DIAGNOSIS, PRESCRIPTION, REPAIR, VERIFICATION)
details.note: 5-layer pipeline matches exactly

details.claim: PART2 §6.9: SDAR LOCK constraints (9 rules including MAX_CONCURRENT_REPAIRS=1, OBSERVATION_PERIOD=300, etc.)
details.note: All 9 LOCK constraints match exactly between PART2 and SDAR_SPEC §9.2

details.claim: PART2 §6.9: Emergency Kill Switch accessible by all RBAC roles (including VIEWER)
details.note: Kill switch activation by all roles, deactivation by ADMIN+, confirmed

details.claim: PART2 §6.9: CATEGORY E security errors - auto-repair absolutely forbidden at any AR-Level
details.note: All 5 rules for CATEGORY E match: no auto-repair, immediate block, audit log, human notification, 30-day data retention

details.claim: PART2 §6.5: V1=2-Layer Guardrails, V2=3-Layer, V3=4-Layer (from overview table line 112)
details.note: D2.0-07 defines 3-Layer (L1+L2+L3) in §1.1 and 4-Layer in §15.8. The version-mapped progression (V1=2, V2=3, V3=4) is a PART2 implementation decision not explicitly in D2.0-07 as a version map. §15.8 mentions 4th layer (C2PA) for V3.

details.claim: PART2 §6.1.3: Custom Hooks 8 (useTauriIPC, useDecision, useWorkflow, useMemory, useCost, useNotification, useAutonomy, useLog)
details.note: v8 Agent 7 found 2 Hook names differ: PART2 has useAutonomy/useLog vs PHASE_B2 has useApproval/useConfig. This was flagged as HIGH in v8. Still unresolved.

step_b.description: Reverse verification: critical SOT facts checked in PART2

details.sot_fact: D2.0-07 S7E-011: Instruction Hierarchy L0~L4 (Core Constitution > System Prompt > User > Tool/Doc > History)
details.note: CRITICAL V1 item not explicitly reflected in PART2 §6.5 or elsewhere. Phase2 F-05 already flagged. PART2 §6.5.4 OWASP LLM01 mentions 'system/user prompt separation' but does not define the 5-level hierarchy.

details.sot_fact: D2.0-07 §4.3.2: P2 HITL timeout is 5 minutes (not 10 minutes)
details.note: PART2 §6.5 only mentions 10min approval timeout. The 5min P2 HITL timeout is not mentioned separately. This is a safety-critical distinction.

details.sot_fact: D2.0-07 community skill security verification 4-stage process (source check, code scan, sandbox, permission declaration)
details.note: Not present in PART2. Phase2 F-08 already flagged as LOW.

details.sot_fact: D2.0-08 §4.4: I-10 UI Orchestration 6-state model (UIS1~6) parallel to 9-state model
details.note: PART2 §6.1.6 covers 9-state model and references D2.0-08 §4 as canonical. The 6-state model is implicitly covered.

details.sot_fact: D2.0-08 §1.5: Approval/Cost/Policy shown as 'behavior constraints' in UI
details.note: Reflected in PART2 §6.1 via ApprovalDialog, CostDashboard, GuardrailsAlert components

details.sot_fact: D2.0-08 §3.1: V1 desktop-only, min-width 1280px, no responsive/mobile
details.note: Implicitly present through V1 scope definition. PART2 doesn't explicitly state min-width but V1 is desktop Tauri app.

details.sot_fact: D2.0-08 §0: i18n with ko-KR default, en-US secondary, ja-JP for V2
details.note: v8 Agent 7 found ja-JP missing from PART2 (HIGH). PART2 §6.1 does not explicitly list ja-JP. This is still a gap.

details.sot_fact: SDAR_SPEC §9.6: P2 domain repair requires human approval regardless of AR-Level
details.note: Reflected in PART2 §6.9 P2 도메인 수리 제한 section

details.sot_fact: SDAR_SPEC §9.7: SDAR repair cost within CostBudget ceiling (V1: 40,000won/mo)
details.note: Reflected in PART2 §6.9 비용 영향 제한 section

details.sot_fact: D2.0-07 §3.1: 2-stage approval structure (plan approval + execution approval)
details.note: Reflected throughout PART2 approval workflows

details.sot_fact: D2.0-07 §3.2.1: Autonomy Level default is L1 (SUPERVISED)
details.note: PART2 §6.5 lists Autonomy Level L0~L3 gating, consistent with SOT
PART2 §6.9 Phase reference matches: V1 OFF, V2 AR-L2/L3, V3 AR-L4 + Self-evo

dim_c.description: Implementation feasibility checks for safety layer + UI integration

details.check: SDAR 5-Gate reuses VAMOS 5-Gate base interfaces (BaseGate ABC)
details.note: PART2 §6.9 explicitly defines Gate 1/3/4 sharing with VAMOS 5-Gate via BaseGate(ABC).check(context)->GateResult. Gate 2 (Risk) and Gate 5 (Verification) are SDAR-specific. This is a clean extension pattern.

details.check: UI 9-state machine transitions are deterministic and complete
details.note: PART2 §6.1.6 defines clear transitions: BOOT->IDLE->EDITING->READY->RUNNING->AWAIT_APPROVAL->PRESENTING->RECOVERY->ARCHIVED. D2.0-08 §4.5 provides bidirectional mapping with 6-state Core model.

details.check: Tauri IPC 72 handlers are implementable with Python-Rust bridge (13 JSON-RPC methods)
details.note: 72 IPC handlers (frontend->Rust) route to 13 JSON-RPC methods (Rust->Python). The ratio (~5.5:1) is reasonable as multiple IPC commands map to single backend methods with different params.

details.check: SDAR Kill Switch is implementable across all RBAC roles
details.note: Kill Switch activation requires no special permissions (VIEWER can activate). Deactivation requires ADMIN+. Auto-activation on ROLLBACK_FAILED is straightforward event-driven logic.

details.check: Guardrails version progression (V1=2-Layer, V2=3-Layer, V3=4-Layer) is feasible
details.note: V1: NeMo(L1) + Guardrails AI(L2). V2: add LlamaGuard(L3, GPU). V3: add L4(C2PA). Each layer is independently deployable. LlamaGuard requires GPU which is V2+ only.

details.check: Hook/Store naming inconsistency (useAutonomy/useLog vs useApproval/useConfig) blocks implementation
details.note: PART2 §6.1.3 hook names (useAutonomy, useLog) differ from PHASE_B2 canonical names (useApproval, useConfig). Implementer must decide which names to use. This was flagged in v8 Agent 7 as HIGH and remains unresolved. Not a hard block but causes confusion.

findings.description: Instruction Hierarchy (S7E-011) L0~L4 prompt priority not reflected in PART2
findings.evidence: D2.0-07 S7E-011 defines 5-level hierarchy (L0 Core Constitution > L1 System > L2 User > L3 Tool/Doc > L4 History) as CRITICAL V1 item. PART2 §6.5.4 OWASP LLM01 mentions prompt separation but not the 5-level structure.

findings.description: P2 HITL 5-minute timeout not separately documented in PART2
findings.evidence: D2.0-07 §2.1 and §4.3.2 define P2 HITL timeout as 5 minutes (vs 10 min general). PART2 §6.5 only mentions 10min approval timeout universally.

findings.description: Guardrails L2 role described as 'output verification' in PART2 vs 'processing defense' in D2.0-07
findings.evidence: PART2 §6.5 L4865: 'Guardrails AI (L2) | 출력 검증'. D2.0-07 §1.1: '2층 — 처리 방어: Guardrails AI — 처리 단계에서 출력 품질/안전성 검증'

findings.description: Hook names inconsistency between PART2 and PHASE_B2 canonical source (still unresolved from v8)
findings.evidence: PART2 §6.1.3: useAutonomy/useLog. PHASE_B2 §3.1: useApproval/useConfig. v8 Agent 7 flagged as HIGH. Store name authStore vs configStore also differs.

findings.description: SDAR state machine uses simplified names in PART2 vs formal prefixed names in SDAR_SPEC
findings.evidence: PART2: IDLE/DETECTING/etc. SDAR_SPEC §7: SDAR_S0_MONITORING/SDAR_S1_DETECTED/etc. PART2 has XREF note acknowledging difference.

findings.description: Community skill security verification 4-stage process not in PART2
findings.evidence: D2.0-07 defines: source check -> code scan -> sandbox execution -> permission declaration. Not reflected in PART2 §6.5.

findings.description: D2.0-08 ja-JP i18n locale not mentioned in PART2 UI sections
findings.evidence: D2.0-08 §0: 'V2 확장 언어: ja-JP'. PART2 §6.1 does not list ja-JP. v8 Agent 7 flagged as HIGH.

v8_comparison.summary: v8 Agent 7 covered UI/UX + multimodal with 14 checks, finding 6 MISMATCH and 5 HIGH issues. This v13 session expanded scope to include Safety/Cost/Approval (D2.0-07) and SDAR (SDAR_SPEC) verification alongside UI/UX. v8's Hook/Store name mismatches (HIGH) are confirmed still unresolved (F-A7-04). v8's ja-JP locale gap is confirmed (F-A7-07). New findings include Instruction Hierarchy L0-L4 missing (F-A7-01) and P2 HITL 5min timeout gap (F-A7-02). SDAR verification shows strong alignment between PART2 §6.9 and SDAR_SPEC (all 9 LOCK constraints match). Overall, v8's HIGH issues on Hook/Store names remain the most actionable gap.
